homeFaq.q1: What is a systems integration company?
homeFaq.a1: A systems integrator is a company specialized in designing, installing, and commissioning complete control and monitoring solutions for industrial plants. Authomathika has been operating as an integrator since 1999, with over 380,000+ automation points across 180 plants in Brazil.
homeFaq.a2: Authomathika serves more than 12 industrial sectors, including sugar and ethanol, fertilizers, sanitation, mining, food and beverage, solar photovoltaic energy, logistics, chemicals, steel, pulp and paper, among others.
homeFaq.q3: What is the difference between greenfield and brownfield projects?
homeFaq.a3: A greenfield project is a completely new installation, where engineering is designed from scratch for a plant yet to be built. A brownfield project involves modernization, expansion, or revamp of existing systems in plants already in operation. Authomathika has experience in both models.
homeFaq.a4: Yes. Authomathika offers EPC (Engineering, Procurement and Construction) contracts, assuming full responsibility from basic engineering to commissioning. Case studies include complete sugar and ethanol plants, sugar factories, and turnkey boilers.
homeFaq.q5: How can I request a quote?
homeFaq.a5: You can request a quote through the form on the Proposal Request page, by phone at +55 16 3513-4000, or via WhatsApp at +55 16 99646-5516. Authomathika offers a free scope study to estimate timelines and costs for each project.

1.question: How does project execution work during the off-season?
1.answer: Authomathika has proven experience in concentrated off-season execution. Detailed planning begins months in advance, with a strict schedule, mobilization of NR-10/NR-35 certified teams and complete logistics management. The goal is to minimize downtime and ensure the plant is ready for the next harvest.

3.question: Is it possible to modernize the plant's automation without stopping production?
3.answer: Yes. Authomathika has expertise in planned automation system migrations with minimal impact on production. We use gradual transition strategies, where the new system operates in parallel with the legacy system until full validation, ensuring operational continuity.

0.question: What is an industrial automation systems integrator?
0.answer: An industrial automation systems integrator is a company specialized in designing, installing and commissioning complete process control and monitoring solutions. Authomathika has been operating as an integrator since 1999, having deployed over 380,000+ automation points across 180 industrial plants in Brazil, working with platforms such as Rockwell PLCs, Siemens, Yokogawa and SCADA systems.

1.question: What is the difference between greenfield and brownfield automation projects?
1.answer: A greenfield project is a completely new implementation, where the automation is designed from scratch for a plant that is yet to be built. A brownfield project involves the modernization, expansion or revamp of existing systems in plants already in operation. Authomathika has experience in both: 49 greenfield plants and 144 brownfield plants in the Automation division.

2.question: Which SCADA systems and PLCs does Authomathika work with?
2.answer: Authomathika works with all major platforms on the market: Rockwell (Allen-Bradley) PLCs, Siemens, Yokogawa, Emerson, Altus, ABB, Schneider and GE. For SCADA, we develop custom software and integrate supervisory systems for real-time monitoring of industrial processes.

3.question: How long does an industrial automation project take?
3.answer: The timeline depends on the project's complexity and scope. A partial automation revamp (brownfield) can take 3 to 6 months, while a complete greenfield implementation can require 8 to 18 months. Authomathika offers a free scope study to estimate timelines and costs for each project.

0.question: What is industrial electrical engineering?
0.answer: Industrial electrical engineering encompasses the design, construction and commissioning of electrical systems for industrial plants, including substations, electrical panels (MCCs, LV switchboards), cogeneration systems and thermoelectric power plants. Authomathika has executed projects in 43 greenfield and 82 brownfield plants, with over 225 MCCs and 256 LV switchboards installed.

1.question: What is the difference between an MCC and an LV switchboard?
1.answer: An MCC (Motor Control Center) is an electrical panel designed to power, protect and control industrial electric motors. An LV switchboard (Low-Voltage Switchboard) is the main low-voltage electrical distribution panel of a facility. Authomathika designs and installs both types of panels, as well as switchgear and capacitor banks.

2.question: What is a cogeneration system?
2.answer: Cogeneration is the process of simultaneously generating electrical and thermal energy from a single fuel source, such as sugarcane bagasse in sugar-energy plants. Authomathika has deployed 23 thermoelectric power plants with cogeneration systems, optimizing energy efficiency and enabling the sale of surplus power to the grid.

3.question: Does Authomathika supply electrical panels?
3.answer: Yes. Authomathika designs, manufactures and installs industrial electrical panels, including MCCs, LV switchboards, switchgear and capacitor banks. We work with leading component manufacturers such as WEG, Rockwell, Siemens, ABB, Schneider, GE and EATON, ensuring compliance with technical standards and NR-10 certifications.

0.question: What is an industrial scheduled shutdown?
0.answer: A scheduled shutdown is a planned interruption of an industrial plant's operation to perform preventive maintenance, instrument calibration, inspections and repairs. Authomathika executes scheduled shutdowns with NR-10 and NR-35 certified teams, having accumulated over 297,000 service hours and 42,027 pieces of equipment serviced.

1.question: How much does an industrial scheduled shutdown cost?
1.answer: The cost varies based on plant size, number of equipment, scope of services and duration. A small-scale shutdown can start from approximately $10,000, while large-scale shutdowns at sugar-energy plants can exceed $100,000. Authomathika provides detailed quotations with a complete technical scope for precise sizing.

2.question: What is RBC metrology and why is it important?
2.answer: RBC (Brazilian Calibration Network) metrology is the instrument calibration system with traceability certified by INMETRO (Brazil's national standards body). It is essential for ensuring that instruments such as pressure, temperature and flow transmitters provide accurate and reliable measurements. Authomathika has both fixed and mobile laboratories with RBC certification.

3.question: What is Authomathika's Serelepe system?
3.answer: Serelepe is Authomathika's proprietary online system for field service management. It provides complete traceability of technical service calls, digital certificate generation and real-time tracking of maintenance and calibration status, ensuring transparency and quality in every service call.

0.question: What is industrial electromechanical assembly?
0.answer: Electromechanical assembly is the integrated installation of electrical and mechanical components in industrial plants, including equipment, piping, structural steel, panels and power and control systems. Authomathika has executed 251 assembly projects, from small to large scale, over more than 25 years of operation.

1.question: Which industries does Authomathika serve in electromechanical assembly?
1.answer: Authomathika serves various industrial sectors: sugar-energy (sugar and ethanol plants), fertilizers, water and sanitation, logistics, mining, food and beverage, chemical and steel. With 105 small-scale, 74 medium-scale and 29 large-scale projects delivered, the company has proven multi-sector experience.

2.question: What is the difference between small, medium and large-scale assembly?
2.answer: The classification depends on the volume of services, complexity and investment. Small-scale projects involve individual equipment or isolated systems. Medium-scale covers complete sections of a plant. Large-scale comprises full implementations, such as a complete turnkey plant. Authomathika has experience at all scales.

0.question: What is EPC engineering?
0.answer: EPC stands for Engineering, Procurement and Construction. It is a contract model where a single company assumes full responsibility for a project: from basic and detailed engineering, through material and equipment procurement, to construction, assembly and commissioning. Authomathika has been delivering EPC contracts since 1999.

1.question: What is the advantage of hiring an EPC project?
1.answer: The EPC model provides a single point of responsibility, which simplifies management, reduces interface risks between suppliers, ensures an integrated schedule and greater cost predictability. For the client, it means having a single point of contact for the entire project, from engineering to operational delivery.

2.question: What is the difference between EPC and EPCM?
2.answer: In EPC, the contractor assumes full responsibility for execution, including costs and deadlines (typically fixed price). In EPCM (Engineering, Procurement, Construction Management), the contractor manages the project but does not directly execute the construction, acting as a manager. Authomathika offers both models, with strategic partnerships for execution when necessary.

3.question: Does Authomathika execute turnkey projects?
3.answer: Yes. Turnkey projects are an Authomathika specialty, where we deliver the plant ready to operate. Case studies include the Costa Rica Plant (complete sugar-energy plant), Rio Vermelho (turnkey sugar factory) and Pedra Buriti (turnkey boiler). All delivered on schedule and within the agreed budget.

0.question: Does Authomathika install solar photovoltaic power plants?
0.answer: Yes. Authomathika's Renewable Energy division has installed Over 890 MW of solar energy across 10 photovoltaic parks, operating in both centralized and distributed generation. Services include electrical and mechanical assembly, civil assembly, executive engineering, commissioning and operations and maintenance (O&M).

1.question: What is the difference between centralized and distributed solar energy generation?
1.answer: Centralized generation (CG) consists of large solar plants, typically above 5 MW, that inject power directly into the transmission grid. Distributed generation (DG) consists of smaller systems, installed on rooftops or consumer premises, connected to the local distribution grid. Authomathika operates in both modalities.

2.question: What does the O&M service for solar plants include?
2.answer: The Operations and Maintenance (O&M) service for solar plants includes remote performance monitoring, periodic inspections, module cleaning, preventive and corrective maintenance of inverters and trackers, alarm management and performance ratio (PR) indicators, and interface with the utility company for connection matters.

3.question: What is Authomathika's solar energy deployment capacity?
3.answer: Authomathika has proven capability for large-scale solar plant deployment. The largest project executed was UFV Belmonte I & II, with 455 MW capacity, in partnership with Grupo Cobra. With 10 parks and over 890 MW total installed, the company has the experience to serve projects above 10 MW.

tecnologia-da-informacao.shortDescription: CCTV, fiber optics and industrial networks
tecnologia-da-informacao.description: Authomathika's IT division offers complete solutions in telecommunications infrastructure and industrial networks, including CCTV systems, fiber optic installation and industrial communication networks.

slv-1a.name: SLV-1A
slv-1a.description: The SLV-1A is an intelligent Donnelly chute level meter developed by Authomathika for the sugar-energy sector. With over 2,685 installed units worldwide, it is a reference in reliability and precision for sugar and ethanol plants.

features.0: Continuous and precise chute level measurement

px-400.name: PX 400
px-400.description: The PX 400 is a pasteurization analyzer and meter with a US patent (US Patent 6,870,486). A high-precision instrument for industrial pasteurization process control.

auth-300.name: AUTH 300
auth-300.description: The AUTH 300 is an electropneumatic cylindrical actuator developed by Authomathika. With over 190 installed units, it offers precision and reliability for industrial flow control applications.

dpg-1a.name: DPG-1A
dpg-1a.description: The DPG-1A is a roller position and displacement transmitter, designed for applications in mills and processing equipment. It provides precise and reliable measurement in harsh industrial environments.

dclt-1c.name: DCLT-1C
dclt-1c.description: The DCLT-1C is the most advanced continuous Donnelly chute level meter in the Authomathika product line. With an OLED display, IP67 protection rating and patent pending status, it represents the state of the art in instrumentation for sugar-energy plants.

features.5: Resistance to humid and harsh environments

promtec.name: ProMtec
promtec.description: The ProMtec is a density and process meter with over 8,800 installations worldwide. Developed with German technology, it is a global reference in density measurement for industrial processes.

derivador-asi.name: AS-i Tap-Off
derivador-asi.description: Authomathika's AS-i Tap-Off is a device for connection and distribution in AS-Interface (AS-i) networks, a global standard for field-level industrial automation communication.

planta-didatica.name: Training Plant
planta-didatica.description: Authomathika's Training Plant is a training and educational equipment developed for hands-on control loop testing. Ideal for technical training of operators and engineers.

paineis.name: Electrical Panels
paineis.description: Authomathika designs and manufactures custom industrial electrical panels, including MCCs, LV switchboards, switchgear and automation panels. Each panel is built according to current technical standards and the client's specific requirements.

linhaDoTempo.title: Timeline
linhaDoTempo.description: From 1999 to today: over 25 years of milestones in integrating engineering. 892 MW solar, 2,000+ days without accidents, ISO 9001 certification and continuous evolution.
linhaDoTempo.subtitle: Milestones in Authomathika's evolution since 1999.

1999.headline: Founding of Authomathika in Sertãozinho, SP
1999.highlight: Beginning operations as an electrical systems and industrial automation integrator

2003.headline: Expansion into thermoelectric and cogeneration plants
2003.highlight: First complete automation projects in cogeneration plants

2005.headline: ISO 9001 Certification obtained
2005.highlight: Quality management system implemented across all processes

2009.headline: Major cogeneration plants in the Midwest

2012.headline: Supplier Excellence Award and fertilizer expansion

2013.headline: Entry into logistics sector — LOGUM ethanol pipelines

2014.headline: First international project — Angola + record 18 projects
2014.highlight: Largest volume of simultaneous projects at the time: Vale Fertilizantes (3 plants), Biocom Angola and logistics expansion.

2015.headline: Launch of the Renewable Energy division — 14 projects delivered
2015.highlight: First photovoltaic solar project. Simultaneous expansion in sanitation, logistics and energy.

2016.headline: 15 MW thermoelectric plant, STP and Bunge cycle expansion

2017.headline: Operations in Panama — second country served

2018.headline: Historical record — 20 simultaneous projects
2018.highlight: Largest volume of operations in company history. Bunge (3 units), São Martinho, Mosaic and first large-scale EPC projects.

2019.headline: 2,000 accident-free days — operational safety milestone
2019.highlight: Historic safety milestone consolidated. 18 projects delivered: São Martinho, FS Bioenergia and sanitation expansion.

2021.headline: UFV Belmonte 300 MW + 20 projects — definitive entry into solar
2021.highlight: Largest solar project executed to date, in partnership with Grupo Cobra. São Martinho (4 units), Mosaic (3 units) and Adimax simultaneously.

2023.headline: UFV São José do Belmonte 300 MW and industrial biogas

2025.headline: UFV Lins I & II — 207 MW in Ceará

2026.headline: UFV Lins III–VIII — 230 MW in Piauí — in progress
2026.highlight: Authomathika's largest solar project in progress. Cristino Castro, PI.

equipe.presidentMessage: Since 1999, I founded Authomathika with a simple principle: deliver on our promises. In over 25 years in the market, we built our reputation delivering critical industrial projects with responsibility, safety and technical excellence. Our team is made up of professionals passionate about engineering, who understand that each project represents the trust the client places in us. We work so that this trust is always repaid with concrete results.
